description: Collections Grid Redesign (Phase 3) - Netflix/Steam-inspired cover grid with sorting, completion %, cover-from-item, and drag-to-reorder

userStories.title: Add CreatedAt and UpdatedAt timestamps to Collection entity
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CreatedAt and UpdatedAt timestamps on the Collection model so the frontend can display relative time metadata and the API can support date-based sorting.
acceptanceCriteria: Add CreatedAt (DateTime, default UTC now) and UpdatedAt (DateTime?, nullable) to the Collection entity
Generate and apply EF Core migration — existing rows get CreatedAt = UTC now, UpdatedAt = null
CreatedAt is set on POST to /api/collections (in CollectionsService.CreateAsync)
UpdatedAt is set on PUT to /api/collections/{id} (in CollectionsService.UpdateAsync)
UpdatedAt is set on cover upload (in CollectionsService.UploadCoverAsync)
Both fields included in CollectionResponse DTO
Existing integration tests still pass (dotnet test)
Typecheck passes (dotnet build)

userStories.title: Propagate UpdatedAt to parent Collection from child services
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CatalogItemsService and OwnedCopiesService to update the parent Collection's UpdatedAt when items are added, removed, or reordered.
acceptanceCriteria: CatalogItemsService.CreateAsync sets UpdatedAt on the parent Collection
CatalogItemsService.DeleteAsync sets UpdatedAt on the parent Collection
CatalogItemsService.ReorderAsync sets UpdatedAt on the parent Collection
OwnedCopiesService.CreateAsync propagates UpdatedAt to the parent Collection (via CatalogItem → Collection)
OwnedCopiesService.DeleteAsync propagates UpdatedAt to the parent Collection (via CatalogItem → Collection)
Existing integration tests still pass (dotnet test)
Typecheck passes (dotnet build)
userStories.notes: For OwnedCopies, the chain is OwnedCopy → CatalogItem → Collection (two hops). Load the parent Collection via the CatalogItem's CollectionId.

userStories.title: Add CollectionTypeName to CollectionResponse and fix N+1 query
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CollectionTypeName in the response DTO and the N+1 item count query eliminated so sorting by itemCount can work at the DB level.
acceptanceCriteria: CollectionTypeName included in CollectionResponse DTO
CollectionsRepository.GetByUserIdAsync() adds .Include(c => c.CollectionType) and maps c.CollectionType.Name
Refactor CollectionsService.GetAllAsync to eliminate N+1 — replace per-collection GetItemCountAsync() loop with a single projected query returning collections with item counts in one DB round-trip
Existing integration tests still pass (dotnet test)
Typecheck passes (dotnet build)
userStories.notes: The projected query pattern (GroupJoin or subquery via .Select()) is needed for US-004 sort-by-itemCount to work.

userStories.title: Add server-side sort to collections API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sort my collections by name, last updated, most items, or recently added.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/collections accepts optional query params: sortBy (name | updatedAt | itemCount | createdAt) and sortDir (asc | desc)
Default sort: name ascending
Sorting is performed in the database query (not in-memory)
The itemCount sort option uses the DB-level count (not fetch all items to count in-memory)
Integration tests cover each sort option (name asc/desc, updatedAt, itemCount, createdAt)
Typecheck passes (dotnet build)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 projected query refactor. Frontend sends ?sortBy=name&sortDir=asc.

userStories.title: Add completion percentage to CollectionResponse
userStories.description: As a developer, I need OwnedCount and CompletionPercentage in the CollectionResponse DTO so the frontend can show collection progress.
acceptanceCriteria: CollectionResponse DTO includes OwnedCount (int) and CompletionPercentage (double, 0-100)
Completion calculated as (OwnedCount / ItemCount) * 100 — reuse the pattern from SetsService
Computed in the same projected query as item count (no extra round-trip)
If ItemCount is 0, CompletionPercentage is 0 (avoid division by zero)
Integration tests verify completion calculation
Typecheck passes (dotnet build)
userStories.notes: Add OwnedCopies.Count() subquery to the existing projected query from US-003.

userStories.title: Add cover-from-item API endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to set a catalog item's image as my collection cover without uploading a separate image.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint: POST /api/collections/{id}/cover-from-item/{itemId}
Copies the item's image file to a new collection cover path (collection-{id}-{timestamp}.{ext})
Image is copied (not referenced) so deleting the catalog item doesn't break the cover
Validates that the item belongs to the collection and the collection belongs to the user
Returns 404 if item has no image
Sets UpdatedAt on the collection
Integration tests for: happy path, item-not-in-collection, item-has-no-image
Typecheck passes (dotnet build)
userStories.notes: Reuse CollectionsService.UploadCoverAsync file-saving logic for the copy operation.

userStories.title: Add SortOrder field and reorder endpoint for collections
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SortOrder field on Collection and a reorder endpoint so the frontend can support drag-to-reorder.
acceptanceCriteria: Add SortOrder (int, default 0) to Collection entity; generate EF Core migration
New endpoint: POST /api/collections/reorder accepting ReorderCollectionsRequest(List<int> CollectionIds)
Follows the existing CatalogItemsService.ReorderAsync pattern
Default sort when sortBy is not specified changes from name to sortOrder
Integration test for reorder endpoint
Typecheck passes (dotnet build)
userStories.notes: Existing rows get SortOrder = 0 (will sort by creation order as tiebreaker).

userStories.title: Remove page subtitle and add i18n keys for toolbar and grid
userStories.description: As a user, I want a cleaner page header without the subtitle, and all new i18n keys ready for the toolbar and grid UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove description prop from PageHeader on collections page (removes 'Manage your collectible collections.')
New Collection button remains in the actions slot, right-aligned

Add new i18n keys to both en.json and pt.json: sort options (collections.sort.name, collections.sort.lastUpdated, collections.sort.mostItems, collections.sort.recentlyAdded), toolbar labels (collections.toolbar.filters, collections.toolbar.sortBy), view toggle tooltips (collections.view.grid, collections.view.list), time-ago patterns (collections.updated, collections.complete), 'Use as Cover' label
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: i18n key collections.description can remain in locale files (no breaking change) but is no longer rendered.

userStories.title: Build collection toolbar with search, type filter, and sort
userStories.description: As a user, I want a unified toolbar with search, type filter, sort dropdown, and CTA button so I can efficiently browse collections.
acceptanceCriteria: Toolbar is a horizontal flex container below the page header
Contains: search input (max-width 420px, search icon), type filter dropdown, sort dropdown, New Collection button (right-aligned via ml-auto)
Search filters collections by name (existing debounced behavior preserved)
Type filter options: All Types (default), plus all collection types from API
Sort dropdown options: Name, Last Updated, Most Items, Recently Added — triggers server-side sort via API query params
Sort default: Name ascending
Sort preference (sortBy + sortDir) persisted to localStorage and restored on page load
New Collection button moves from header actions slot into the toolbar (right side)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Uses i18n keys from US-008. Sort calls GET /api/collections?sortBy=X&sortDir=Y from US-004.

userStories.title: Collapsible mobile filter toolbar
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want filters hidden behind a Filters toggle so the toolbar doesn't consume too much vertical space.
acceptanceCriteria: On mobile (< sm breakpoint): search input shown full-width; a Filters toggle button shown next to it
Tapping Filters reveals/hides the type filter + sort dropdown row with smooth animation
New Collection button remains always visible
On desktop (>= sm): all toolbar items shown inline, no toggle needed
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Uses i18n key collections.toolbar.filters from US-008.

userStories.title: Build CollectionCoverCard component
userStories.description: As a user, I want each collection displayed as a visual cover tile (Netflix-style) so I can visually identify collections at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: New CollectionCoverCard component replaces current card markup
Card structure: full-bleed cover image (aspect-ratio 4/3, object-fit cover) with gradient overlay at bottom
Overlay contains: collection name (18px, semibold, white), metadata line (13px, white/85% opacity)
Gradient overlay: linear-gradient(transparent, rgba(0,0,0,0.7))
Fallback when no cover image: gradient placeholder with Library icon
Entire card is clickable — navigates to /collections/{id}
Card has border-radius 12px (rounded-xl) and overflow hidden
Remove: View button, CardFooter, CardContent wrapper, type Badge from card body
Cover images use loading='lazy' for off-screen cards
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Metadata line content depends on US-012. For now show '{count} items'.

userStories.title: Card metadata line with completion % and relative time
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see item count, completion percentage, and last updated time on each collection cover.
acceptanceCriteria: Metadata line in overlay: '{count} items · {percent}% complete · Updated {timeAgo}'
Time displayed as relative (e.g. '2 days ago', 'just now') using Intl.RelativeTimeFormat or lightweight utility
If updatedAt is null, show only item count and completion %
If ItemCount is 0, show '0 items' without completion %
Metadata styled: 13px, white text, 85% opacity
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Uses CompletionPercentage from backend US-005 and UpdatedAt from US-001. Do not add date-fns — use Intl.RelativeTimeFormat.

userStories.title: Card hover interactions and quick actions
userStories.description: As a user, I want hover feedback and quick action buttons on collection cover cards.
acceptanceCriteria: On hover: card lifts with translateY(-4px) and increased box-shadow
On hover: quick action buttons appear over the cover (Open, Edit, three-dot menu)
Open navigates to collection detail, Edit opens the edit dialog
Three-dot menu provides Delete option (existing behavior)
Quick actions have a semi-transparent backdrop for readability
On touch devices, the three-dot menu is always visible
Transition duration ~200ms ease
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Responsive grid layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the collection grid to show 3 columns on desktop, 2 on tablet, and 1 on mobile.
acceptanceCriteria: Grid container uses CSS grid with repeat(auto-fill, minmax(320px, 1fr)) and gap 24px
Effectively shows ~3 cols desktop, ~2 tablet, 1 mobile
Page padding 32px or equivalent Tailwind spacing
Stagger animation preserved (Framer Motion StaggerChildren)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Grid vs list view toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between a visual cover grid and a compact list view.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle button (grid icon / list icon) in the toolbar, next to sort dropdown
Grid view: cover tile layout (default)
List view: compact table/row layout using DataTable DS component showing collection name, type, item count, updated date
Active view persisted to localStorage (key: collections-view-mode), restored on page load
Default: grid view
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Skeleton loading state for cover grid
userStories.description: As a user, I want skeleton placeholders that match the new cover card shape while collections load.
acceptanceCriteria: Loading state shows 6 skeleton cards in the same grid layout
Each skeleton: aspect-ratio 4/3, rounded-xl, animated pulse
Skeleton includes faint overlay area at bottom to mimic text placement
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Use as Cover option to catalog item context menu
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pick a catalog item's image as the collection cover from the collection detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: On the collection detail page: add 'Use as Cover' option to the catalog item's context menu / three-dot dropdown
Clicking 'Use as Cover' calls POST /api/collections/{id}/cover-from-item/{itemId} from US-006
Shows success toast on completion
Collection cover updates immediately in the detail page header
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Uses the API endpoint from US-006.

userStories.title: Drag-to-reorder collections in grid view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag collections to reorder them in the grid view.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrap grid in SortableList component (already exists in DS) in grid view only
Drag handle visible on hover (GripVertical icon, same as collection-detail-page items)
Reorder calls POST /api/collections/reorder from US-007 with optimistic UI update and rollback on error
When user selects an explicit sort option (name, updatedAt, etc.), drag reorder is disabled and a visual indicator shows sorting is active
Drag-to-reorder disabled in list view
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Uses SortableList from DS and reorder endpoint from US-007.

userStories.title: Update existing tests for new collections page UI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need existing tests updated and new tests added for the redesigned collections page.
acceptanceCriteria: All existing test scenarios in collections-page.test.tsx updated to work with new markup
Tests cover: cover card rendering, card click navigation, hover quick actions, toolbar search/filter/sort, empty state, create/edit/delete dialogs
New test for sort dropdown triggering API call with query params
New test for mobile filter toggle
New test for grid/list view toggle and localStorage persistence
New test for completion percentage display on card
New test for drag-to-reorder (SortableList interaction)
All frontend tests pass (npm test)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
